 276 - ```

 277 - firstcocoagent/

 278 - ├── CLAUDE.md ← This file (project-wide rules)

 279 - ├── SCOUT_HISTORY.md ← Bug log + changelog (not loaded each session)

 280 - ├── Procfile ← "web: python -m agent.main"

 281 - ├── requirements.txt

 282 - ├── agent/

 283 - │ ├── CLAUDE.md ← Module APIs for agent/ files

 284 - │ ├── main.py ← Entry point. Scheduler poll loop. All tool dispatch.

 285 - │ ├── config.py ← Env vars

 286 - │ ├── claude_brain.py ← Claude API + tool definitions + memory injection

 287 - │ ├── memory_manager.py ← Persistent memory: read/write/GitHub commit

 288 - │ ├── scheduler.py ← CST-aware Scheduler class, check() only

 289 - │ ├── keywords.py ← Lead research title/keyword list

 290 - │ ├── voice_trainer.py ← Paginated email fetch + paired context analysis

 291 - │ ├── call_processor.py ← Transcript → summary → Google Doc

 292 - │ └── webhook_server.py ← aiohttp server for Fireflies webhook

 293 - ├── tools/

 294 - │ ├── CLAUDE.md ← Module APIs for tools/ files

 295 - │ ├── telegram_bot.py

 296 - │ ├── research_engine.py ← Research Job, Research Queue (singleton: research_queue)

 297 - │ ├── contact_extractor.py

 298 - │ ├── sheets_writer.py ← MODULE not class. write_contacts(), count_leads(), etc.

 299 - │ ├── gas_bridge.py ← GASBridge class

 300 - │ ├── github_pusher.py ← push_file(), list_repo_files(), get_file_content()

 301 - │ ├── sequence_builder.py ← build_sequence(), write_sequence_to_doc()

 302 - │ ├── activity_tracker.py ← MODULE not class. log_activity(), sync_gmail_activities()

 303 - │ ├── csv_importer.py ← MODULE not class. import_accounts(), classify_account()

 304 - │ ├── daily_call_list.py ← MODULE not class. build_daily_call_list()

 305 - │ ├── district_prospector.py ← MODULE not class. discover_districts(), suggest_upward_targets()

 306 - │ ├── lead_importer.py ← MODULE not class. import_leads(), import_contacts(), enrich

 307 - │ ├── proximity_engine.py ← MODULE not class. C5: proximity search, ESA mapping

 308 - │ ├── signal_processor.py ← MODULE not class. Signal intelligence: Gmail parsing, classification, scoring

 309 - │ ├── email_drafter.py ← MODULE not class. Auto-draft Gmail replies in Steven's voice

 310 - │ ├── charter_prospector.py ← F6 MODULE. Charter CMO seed list loader + queue functions

 311 - │ ├── cte_prospector.py ← F7 MODULE. CTE center seed list loader + queue functions

 312 - │ ├── private_schools.py ← F8 MODULE. Private school Serper discovery + diocesan/chain seed

 313 - │ ├── compliance_gap_scanner.py ← F9 MODULE. Serper PDF + Claude Sonnet document input (CA/IL/MA pilot)

 314 - │ └── fireflies.py ← Fireflies Client, Fireflies Error

 315 - ├── gas/

 316 - │ ├── CLAUDE.md ← GAS deployment checklist and gotchas

 317 - │ └── Code.gs ← Deployed at script.google.com as "Scout Bridge"

 318 - ├── prompts/

 319 - │ ├── system.md

 320 - │ ├── morning_brief.md

 321 - │ ├── eod_report.md

 322 - │ ├── sequence_templates.md ← 18 archetypes

 323 - │ └── reply_draft.md ← Email drafting workflow instructions

 324 - ├── memory/

 325 - │ ├── preferences.md

 326 - │ ├── context_summary.md

 327 - │ ├── voice_profile.md

 328 - │ ├── response_playbook.md ← 14 reply categories from 150+ emails

 329 - │ ├── draft_log.md ← Draft tracking for learning loop

 340 - ## RAILWAY ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES

 341 -

 342 - | Variable | Notes |

 343 - |----------|-------|

 344 - | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | Claude API |

 345 - | TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN | Bot token |

 346 - | TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID | 8677984089 |

 347 - | MORNING_BRIEF_TIME | 09:15 |

 348 - | EOD_REPORT_TIME | 16:30 |

 349 - | TIMEZONE | America/Chicago |

 350 - | AGENT_NAME | Scout |

 351 - | GITHUB_TOKEN | Fine-grained PAT, contents:write |

 352 - | GITHUB_REPO | scadkin/firstcocoagent |

 353 - | CHECKIN_START_HOUR | 10 |

 354 - | CHECKIN_END_HOUR | 16 |

 355 - | SERPER_API_KEY | serper.dev |

 356 - | GOOGLE_SHEETS_ID | From Sheet URL |

 357 - | GOOGLE_SHEETS_TERRITORY_ID | Separate sheet for territory data (falls back to main sheet) |

 358 - | GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON | Full JSON string, personal account |

 359 - | GAS_WEBHOOK_URL | **Update every time Code.gs gets new deployment** |

 360 - | GAS_SECRET_TOKEN | Must match Code.gs |

 361 - | FIREFLIES_API_KEY | app.fireflies.ai → Account → API |

 362 - | FIREFLIES_WEBHOOK_SECRET | Must match Fireflies webhook config |

 363 - | PRECALL_BRIEF_FOLDER_ID | Google Drive folder ID |

 364 - | SEQUENCES_FOLDER_ID | Google Drive folder ID. Paste full browser URL — query params stripped automatically. |

 365 -

 366 - **Note:** Phase 5+ env vars (`FIREFLIES_API_KEY`, `FIREFLIES_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, `PRECALL_BRIEF_FOLDER_ID`, `SEQUENCES_FOLDER_ID`) are read via `os.environ.get()` directly — NOT in `config.py`.

 370 - ## CLAUDE TOOLS (25 total, defined in claude_brain.py, handled in main.py)

 371 -

 372 - `research_district`, `get_sheet_status`, `get_research_queue_status`, `train_voice`, `draft_email`, `save_draft_to_gmail`, `get_calendar`, `log_call`, `create_district_deck`, `push_code`, `list

 - _repo_files`, `get_file_content`, `build_sequence`, `ping_gas_bridge`, `grade_draft`, `add_template`, `process_call_transcript`, `get_pre_call_brief`, `get_activity_summary`, `get_accounts_stat

 375 -

 376 - ## SHORTHAND COMMANDS (handle_message in main.py)

 377 -

 378 - | Command | Action |

 379 - |---------|--------|

 380 - | `research [district], [state]`, `look up [district] in [state]` | direct dispatch to research_district — bypasses Claude |

 381 - | `proximity [state] [miles]`, `nearby districts in [state]` | find districts near active accounts (C5) |

 382 - | `esa [state]`, `service centers in [state]` | ESA/ESC region opportunities (C5) |

 383 - | `/signals` | top 5 district signals by heat score |

 384 - | `/signals all`, `/signals [state]`, `/signals new` | all signals, state filter, new only |

 385 - | `/signal_info N` | full detail on signal #N from last `/signals` list |

 386 - | `/signal_act N` | fast-path: mark acted → add to Prospecting Queue → auto-research |

 387 - | `/signal_dismiss N` | archive signal (hidden from default view) |

 388 - | `/signal_scan` | trigger manual signal scan of Gmail |

 389 - | `/signal_stats` | signal counts by type and state |

 390 - | `/signal_rss` | manual RSS-only scan (K-12 Dive, eSchool News, CSTA) |

 391 - | `/signal_board` | manual BoardDocs agenda scan (25 districts) |

 392 - | `/signal_bonds` | manual Ballotpedia bond measure scan |

 393 - | `/signal_leadership` | manual superintendent change scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 394 - | `/signal_rfp` | manual CS/STEM RFP opportunity scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 395 - | `/signal_legislation` | manual CS/STEM education legislation scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 396 - | `/signal_grants` | manual CS/STEM grant-funded district scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 397 - | `/signal_budget` | manual budget cycle/procurement signal scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 398 - | `/signal_algebra` | AI Algebra campaign: find districts adopting math/algebra curriculum (~$0.03) |

 399 - | `/signal_cyber` | Cybersecurity pre-launch: find districts with CTE cyber programs (~$0.03) |

 400 - | `/prospect_lookalike [state]` | find districts demographically similar to best customers ($0) |

 401 - | `/prospect_reengagement` | scan ALL Outreach sequences for finished/no-reply prospects |

 402 - | `/signal_roles [state]` | find CS/CTE/STEM leaders via Serper (~$2.50/scan, on-demand) |

 403 - | `/signal_csta` | find CSTA chapter leaders/members (~$1.20/scan, on-demand) |

 404 - | `/dormant [days]` | show accounts with past activity that went silent (default 90 days) |

 405 - | `/export_sequence [name]` | export Outreach sequence to Google Doc |

 406 - | `/ping_gas`, `ping gas`, `test gas` | ping GAS bridge |

 407 - | `/train_voice`, `train voice` | train voice from Gmail (24 months) |

 408 - | `/grade_draft`, `grade draft` | feedback on last draft → updates voice_profile.md |

 409 - | `/add_template [content]` | add template to voice profile |

 410 - | `/list_files`, `/ls` | list repo files |

 411 - | `/push_code [filepath]` | fetch-first: read file, ask for changes, edit + push |

 412 - | `/build_sequence [name]` | hybrid: Claude asks questions, then builds + sends directly |

 413 - | `looks good`, `save it`, `approved` | save pending draft to Gmail |

 414 - | `add email: addr@domain.org` | set recipient on pending draft |

 415 - | `/brief [meeting name]` | manual pre-call brief |

 416 - | `/recent_calls [num]` | recent external calls (1–20) |

 417 - | `/call [id] [email]` | post-call processing. Optional email override. |

 418 - | `/progress` or `/kpi` | today's activity vs KPI goals |

 419 - | `/sync_activities` | scan Gmail for PandaDoc + Dialpad events |

 420 - | `/set_goal [type] [target]` | update KPI target |

 421 - | `/call_list [N]` | generate daily call list (default 10, max 50) |

 422 - | `/color_leads` | recolor Leads tab rows by email confidence |

 423 - | `/eod` | manually trigger end-of-day report (useful on weekends) |

 424 - | `/draft_emails`, `draft my emails` | manually trigger email auto-drafting (also runs every 5 min during business hours) |

 425 - | `/draft [name]` | force-draft a specific sender's unread email, bypassing Haiku classification (e.g. `/draft Allison`) |

 426 - | `/list_charter_cmos [state]` | read-only view of the 43-CMO seed list (F6) |

 427 - | `/prospect_charter_cmos [state]` | queue charter CMOs from the seed list as `charter_cmo` strategy prospects (F6) |

 428 - | `/list_cte_centers [state]` | read-only view of the 79-CTE-center seed list (F7) |

 429 - | `/prospect_cte_centers [state]` | queue CTE centers from the seed list as `cte_center` strategy prospects (F7) |

 430 - | `/discover_coops [state]` | F10 Serper-based homeschool co-op discovery for a state |

 431 - | `/discover_private_schools [state]` | F8 Serper-based private school discovery for a state |

 432 - | `/prospect_private_networks [state]` | F8 queue the 24 diocesan/chain networks as `private_school_network` strategy |

 433 - | `/scan_compliance [state]` | F9 PDF pilot — CS graduation compliance gap scan. Pilot states: CA, IL, MA. Cost ~$0.50-$2/scan. |

 434 - | `/prospect_discover [state]` | cold district search via Serper |

 435 - | `/prospect_upward` | upward targets from active accounts |

 436 - | `/prospect` | show next 5 pending districts |

 437 - | `/prospect_all` | full queue grouped by status |

 438 - | `/prospect_winback` | scan Closed Lost tab for winback targets (last 12 months) |

 439 - | `/prospect_add [name], [state]` | manually add district to queue |

 440 - | `/prospect_approve 1,3,5` | approve from last batch, auto-queue research |

 441 - | `/prospect_skip 2,4` | skip from last batch |

 442 - | `/prospect_clear` | wipe all entries from Prospecting Queue tab |

 443 - | `/import_clear` | next CSV upload will clear & rewrite (then resets to merge) |

 444 - | `/import_merge` | switch CSV upload back to merge mode (default) |

 445 - | `/import_replace_state CA` | next CSV upload replaces only that state's rows; all other states untouched (then resets) |

 446 - | `/dedup_accounts` | Remove duplicate rows from Active Accounts tab (uses Name Key + State composite key — fixed Session 18) |

 447 - | `/import_leads` | next CSV upload imports as Salesforce leads (SF Leads tab) |

 448 - | `/import_contacts` | next CSV upload imports as Salesforce contacts (SF Contacts tab) |

 449 - | `/enrich_leads` | run Serper enrichment on unenriched SF Leads (add `contacts` arg for SF Contacts) |

 450 - | `/clear_leads` | clear SF Leads + Leads Assoc Active Accounts data rows + shrink grid |

 451 - | `/clear_contacts` | clear SF Contacts + Contacts Assoc Active Accounts data rows + shrink grid |

 452 - | `/territory_sync [state]` | download NCES territory data for one state or all |

 453 - | `/territory_stats [state]` | territory coverage summary (districts, schools, enrollment) |

 454 - | `/territory_gaps <state>` | gap analysis: cross-ref territory vs Active Accounts + Prospecting Queue |

 455 - | `/territory_map [state]` | generate interactive Folium territory map → Telegram file attachment |

 456 - | `/pipeline` | show open pipeline summary with stale alerts |

 457 - | `/pipeline_import` | next CSV upload imports as opportunities (Pipeline tab) |

 458 - | `/import_closed_lost` | next CSV upload imports as closed-lost opps (Closed Lost tab) |

 459 - | send a `.csv` file | Auto-detects opp vs lead vs contact vs account CSV; or Salesforce active accounts import (merge by default) |

 460 - | describe CSV before upload | "these are my salesforce leads" / "contacts from salesforce" / "pipeline opps" — sets routing for next CSV upload |

 461 - | caption on CSV upload | Same as above — type description as caption when sending the file |

 Now picking up where you actually wanted to start:

 🔔 Reminder before anything else: The Tulsa PS Gmail draft to Robert F. Burton is sitting in your Gmail Drafts folder from Session 52 — Prop 3 ($104.785M tech bond) passed 80.97% on April 7. It uses the
 approved bond-trigger tone (no dollar figures, no peer name-drops, CS/coding + safe AI framing, 3-CTA close). Review it in Drafts and either send or revise before we get deep into Stage 4.

 Next: Railway health check. I'm about to pull the latest Railway deployment status and confirm it's running commit efb0ff8 (the Session 52 Checkpoint A wrap commit). I'll use the Railway API token from
 reference_railway_api.md memory rather than asking you to copy-paste anything.

 After that returns green, here's the Stage 4 sequence I'll walk you through — I will NOT fire any of these until you confirm each one:

 1. /signal_funding — F4 funding scanner. Spot-check gates: (a) does the Source URL column populate on every row, (b) do auto-queued HIGH-confidence rows show non-zero Est. Enrollment in the queue (proof Commit
 1's add_district(**kwargs) fix is live in production), (c) any obvious hallucinations in the first 3 rows.

 2. /signal_competitors — F2 competitor displacement. Spot-check first 3 HIGH signals against the BoardDocs URLs they cite. Session 51 had a bug where this scanner was citing vendor marketing pages — Session 52
 didn't touch F2's evidence rules so we want eyes on real output.

 3. /signal_csta — F5 CSTA chapter leader scan. Spot-check that auto-queued chapter leaders are real CSTA people (not generic CS teachers) and that evidence_type normalization (Commit 4) is keeping the right
 rows.

 Each scan costs ~$0.30-$0.50, total Stage 4 budget ~$1.30. Each one runs in the background on Railway and posts results back to Telegram.

 Want me to kick off the Railway health check now? Just say "go" and I'll pull the deployment status. Don't say go on the scanners yet — we'll do those one at a time after the health check is green and you've
 reviewed the Tulsa draft.

 Now — the Robert Burton draft

 Now — the Robert Burton draft

 I see what's wrong, and it's worse than just visual: the draft body was saved as plain text containing literal HTML ( <br> tags + &#39; entities showing through). On top of that, the Outreach Chrome extension
 is hijacking the Gmail compose window for robert.burton@tulsaschools.org because he's a contact in Outreach — that orange "Prospect will be created when sending email" banner confirms it. This is the
 documented "Outreach extension strips draft body for contacts in Outreach" issue from CLAUDE.md, but with an extra layer: the underlying Gmail draft itself is malformed.

 Root cause hypothesis: When I created the draft via GAS bridge in Session 52, I almost certainly called gas.create_draft(to, subject, body=...) and passed an HTML-formatted body to the plain-text body
 parameter, when GAS's create Draft needs html Body=... for HTML content. Gmail then stored the literal <br> and &#39; strings as plain text, and any client (Gmail web, Outreach extension) renders them literally.

 Before I fix anything, I want to verify two things and propose options — not act:

 1. Verify the GAS create Draft signature so I know whether the right fix is html Body= , a different param name, or "rewrite as plain text with real line breaks." I'll read tools/gas_bridge.py and gas/Code.gs .

 2. Confirm the original approved copy from Session 52 so the rewrite uses the exact tone Steven approved (not a paraphrase).

 Then I'll come back with two options:

 - Option A: Delete the broken draft via GAS delete_draft and recreate it as a clean plain-text draft (real \n\n paragraph breaks, no HTML at all). Safest — bypasses both the HTML issue AND the Outreach
 extension's HTML mangling. Recommended.

 - Option B: Recreate as proper HTML via the right GAS param. Looks slightly nicer in Gmail web, but the Outreach extension is still going to fight us for this contact.

 - Option A: Delete the broken draft via GAS delete_draft and recreate it as a clean plain-text draft (real \n\n paragraph breaks, no HTML at all). Safest — bypasses both the HTML issue AND the Outreach
 extension's HTML mangling. Recommended.
 - Option B: Recreate as prop r HTML via the right GAS param. Looks slightly nicer in Gmail web, but the Outreach extension is still going to fight us for this contact.

 You'll pick A or B, then I do it. Sound good — should I read the GAS bridge code and pull the original draft body now?

 Step 1 — You delete the broken draft (10-second manual)

 Open Gmail Drafts, find the "Congrats on the Prop 3 win" draft to robert.burton@tulsaschools.org, click trash. One click, zero risk. I'd write a programmatic delete but gas_bridge.py doesn't expose
 delete_draft or list_drafts yet, and adding both methods + finding the draft_id by subject is more code than this is worth right now.

 Step 2 — I create a fresh plain-text draft via GAS

 Same recipient, same subject, body below. Plain text means real \n\n paragraph breaks, no <br> , no HTML entities. The Outreach extension can't mangle plain text, and Gmail web will render it cleanly. Here's
 the proposed body — verbatim, this is what I'll send to GAS:

 Robert,

 Steven at CodeCombat. We power CS/coding & safe AI curriculum for ~1,300 K-12 districts — Python, JS, AI, CTE pathways, all standards-aligned, teachers don't need a CS background to run it. Since a chunk of
 Prop 3 is earmarked for software subscriptions, figured I'd throw our hat in early while you're mapping out the RFP and vendor list.

 A few things I can do, no commitment either way:

 - Send over a one pager overview.
 - Give out free trial licenses to any teachers you want to pilot with, no strings.

 - Share some data, a few case studies, or efficacy reports.

 Best,
 Steven

 —

 Steven Adkins

 Senior Partnership Manager

 CodeCombat / Ozaria
 (415) 854-9198

 Subject: Congrats on the Prop 3 win
 To: robert.burton@tulsaschools.org
 CC: none

 thread_id: none (cold outreach, brand new thread)
 content_type: none (= plain text)

 Things I want you to eyeball before I hit go:

 1. Phone number format — I wrote (415) 854-9198 . Your screenshot showed 415.854.9198 . Which do you prefer in cold email signatures?

 2. Signature title — "Senior Partnership Manager" vs. the screenshot's "Ozaria Senior Partnership Manager". Memory says "CodeCombat / Ozaria" combined, but I want to confirm the title line.

 3. Body itself — anything you want cut, added, or rephrased? This follows the approved bond-trigger tone pattern from the memory file (no dollar figure, no named peer districts, three-CTA close, "& safe AI"
 framing).

 Tell me (a) yes or no on each of those, (b) when the broken draft is in your trash, and I'll create the clean version. After that we get back to Stage 4 scanners.

 376 - ## SHORTHAND COMMANDS (handle_message in main.py)

 377 -

 378 - | Command | Action |

 379 - |---------|--------|

 380 - | `research [district], [state]`, `look up [district] in [state]` | direct dispatch to research_district — bypasses Claude |

 381 - | `proximity [state] [miles]`, `nearby districts in [state]` | find districts near active accounts (C5) |

 382 - | `esa [state]`, `service centers in [state]` | ESA/ESC region opportunities (C5) |

 383 - | `/signals` | top 5 district signals by heat score |

 384 - | `/signals all`, `/signals [state]`, `/signals new` | all signals, state filter, new only |

 385 - | `/signal_info N` | full detail on signal #N from last `/signals` list |

 386 - | `/signal_act N` | fast-path: mark acted → add to Prospecting Queue → auto-research |

 387 - | `/signal_dismiss N` | archive signal (hidden from default view) |

 388 - | `/signal_scan` | trigger manual signal scan of Gmail |

 389 - | `/signal_stats` | signal counts by type and state |

 390 - | `/signal_rss` | manual RSS-only scan (K-12 Dive, eSchool News, CSTA) |

 391 - | `/signal_board` | manual BoardDocs agenda scan (25 districts) |

 392 - | `/signal_bonds` | manual Ballotpedia bond measure scan |

 393 - | `/signal_leadership` | manual superintendent change scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 394 - | `/signal_rfp` | manual CS/STEM RFP opportunity scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 395 - | `/signal_legislation` | manual CS/STEM education legislation scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 396 - | `/signal_grants` | manual CS/STEM grant-funded district scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 397 - | `/signal_budget` | manual budget cycle/procurement signal scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 398 - | `/signal_algebra` | AI Algebra campaign: find districts adopting math/algebra curriculum (~$0.03) |

 399 - | `/signal_cyber` | Cybersecurity pre-launch: find districts with CTE cyber programs (~$0.03) |

 400 - | `/prospect_lookalike [state]` | find districts demographically similar to best customers ($0) |

 401 - | `/prospect_reengagement` | scan ALL Outreach sequences for finished/no-reply prospects |

 402 - | `/signal_roles [state]` | find CS/CTE/STEM leaders via Serper (~$2.50/scan, on-demand) |

 403 - | `/signal_csta` | find CSTA chapter leaders/members (~$1.20/scan, on-demand) |

 404 - | `/dormant [days]` | show accounts with past activity that went silent (default 90 days) |

 405 - | `/export_sequence [name]` | export Outreach sequence to Google Doc |

 406 - | `/ping_gas`, `ping gas`, `test gas` | ping GAS bridge |

 407 - | `/train_voice`, `train voice` | train voice from Gmail (24 months) |

 408 - | `/grade_draft`, `grade draft` | feedback on last draft → updates voice_profile.md |

 409 - | `/add_template [content]` | add template to voice profile |

 410 - | `/list_files`, `/ls` | list repo files |

 411 - | `/push_code [filepath]` | fetch-first: read file, ask for changes, edit + push |

 412 - | `/build_sequence [name]` | hybrid: Claude asks questions, then builds + sends directly |

 413 - | `looks good`, `save it`, `approved` | save pending draft to Gmail |

 414 - | `add email: addr@domain.org` | set recipient on pending draft |

 415 - | `/brief [meeting name]` | manual pre-call brief |

 416 - | `/recent_calls [num]` | recent external calls (1–20) |

 417 - | `/call [id] [email]` | post-call processing. Optional email override. |

 418 - | `/progress` or `/kpi` | today's activity vs KPI goals |

 419 - | `/sync_activities` | scan Gmail for PandaDoc + Dialpad events |

 420 - | `/set_goal [type] [target]` | update KPI target |

 421 - | `/call_list [N]` | generate daily call list (default 10, max 50) |

 422 - | `/color_leads` | recolor Leads tab rows by email confidence |

 423 - | `/eod` | manually trigger end-of-day report (useful on weekends) |

 424 - | `/draft_emails`, `draft my emails` | manually trigger email auto-drafting (also runs every 5 min during business hours) |

 425 - | `/draft [name]` | force-draft a specific sender's unread email, bypassing Haiku classification (e.g. `/draft Allison`) |

 426 - | `/list_charter_cmos [state]` | read-only view of the 43-CMO seed list (F6) |

 427 - | `/prospect_charter_cmos [state]` | queue charter CMOs from the seed list as `charter_cmo` strategy prospects (F6) |

 428 - | `/list_cte_centers [state]` | read-only view of the 79-CTE-center seed list (F7) |

 429 - | `/prospect_cte_centers [state]` | queue CTE centers from the seed list as `cte_center` strategy prospects (F7) |

 430 - | `/discover_coops [state]` | F10 Serper-based homeschool co-op discovery for a state |

 431 - | `/discover_private_schools [state]` | F8 Serper-based private school discovery for a state |

 432 - | `/prospect_private_networks [state]` | F8 queue the 24 diocesan/chain networks as `private_school_network` strategy |

 433 - | `/scan_compliance [state]` | F9 PDF pilot — CS graduation compliance gap scan. Pilot states: CA, IL, MA. Cost ~$0.50-$2/scan. |

 434 - | `/prospect_discover [state]` | cold district search via Serper |

 435 - | `/prospect_upward` | upward targets from active accounts |

 436 - | `/prospect` | show next 5 pending districts |

 437 - | `/prospect_all` | full queue grouped by status |

 438 - | `/prospect_winback` | scan Closed Lost tab for winback targets (last 12 months) |

 439 - | `/prospect_add [name], [state]` | manually add district to queue |

 440 - | `/prospect_approve 1,3,5` | approve from last batch, auto-queue research |

 441 - | `/prospect_skip 2,4` | skip from last batch |

 442 - | `/prospect_clear` | wipe all entries from Prospecting Queue tab |

 443 - | `/import_clear` | next CSV upload will clear & rewrite (then resets to merge) |

 444 - | `/import_merge` | switch CSV upload back to merge mode (default) |

 445 - | `/import_replace_state CA` | next CSV upload replaces only that state's rows; all other states untouched (then resets) |

 446 - | `/dedup_accounts` | Remove duplicate rows from Active Accounts tab (uses Name Key + State composite key — fixed Session 18) |

 447 - | `/import_leads` | next CSV upload imports as Salesforce leads (SF Leads tab) |

 448 - | `/import_contacts` | next CSV upload imports as Salesforce contacts (SF Contacts tab) |

 449 - | `/enrich_leads` | run Serper enrichment on unenriched SF Leads (add `contacts` arg for SF Contacts) |

 450 - | `/clear_leads` | clear SF Leads + Leads Assoc Active Accounts data rows + shrink grid |

 451 - | `/clear_contacts` | clear SF Contacts + Contacts Assoc Active Accounts data rows + shrink grid |

 452 - | `/territory_sync [state]` | download NCES territory data for one state or all |

 453 - | `/territory_stats [state]` | territory coverage summary (districts, schools, enrollment) |

 454 - | `/territory_gaps <state>` | gap analysis: cross-ref territory vs Active Accounts + Prospecting Queue |

 455 - | `/territory_map [state]` | generate interactive Folium territory map → Telegram file attachment |

 456 - | `/pipeline` | show open pipeline summary with stale alerts |

 457 - | `/pipeline_import` | next CSV upload imports as opportunities (Pipeline tab) |

 458 - | `/import_closed_lost` | next CSV upload imports as closed-lost opps (Closed Lost tab) |

 459 - | send a `.csv` file | Auto-detects opp vs lead vs contact vs account CSV; or Salesforce active accounts import (merge by default) |

 460 - | describe CSV before upload | "these are my salesforce leads" / "contacts from salesforce" / "pipeline opps" — sets routing for next CSV upload |

 461 - | caption on CSV upload | Same as above — type description as caption when sending the file |

 ---

 Now — the Robert Burton draft

 I see what's wrong, and it's worse than just visual: the draft body was saved as plain text containing literal HTML ( <br> tags + &#39; entities showing through). On top of that, the Outreach Chrome extension

 is hijacking the Gmail compose window for robert.burton@tulsaschools.org because he's a contact in Outreach — that orange "Prospect will be created when sending email" banner confirms it. This is the

 documented "Outreach extension strips draft body for contacts in Outreach" issue from CLAUDE.md, but with an extra layer: the underlying Gmail draft itself is malformed.

 Root cause hypothesis: When I created the draft via GAS bridge in Session 52, I almost certainly called gas.create_draft(to, subject, body=...) and passed an HTML-formatted body to the plain-text body

 parameter, when GAS's create Draft needs html Body=... for HTML content. Gmail then stored the literal <br> and &#39; strings as plain text, and any client (Gmail web, Outreach extension) renders them literally.

 Before I fix anything, I want to verify two things and propose options — not act:

 1. Verify the GAS create Draft signature so I know whether the right fix is html Body= , a different param name, or "rewrite as plain text with real line breaks." I'll read tools/gas_bridge.py and gas/Code.gs .

 2. Confirm the original approved copy from Session 52 so the rewrite uses the exact tone Steven approved (not a paraphrase).

 Then I'll come back with two options:

 - Option A: Delete the broken draft via GAS delete_draft and recreate it as a clean plain-text draft (real \n\n paragraph breaks, no HTML at all). Safest — bypasses both the HTML issue AND the Outreach

 extension's HTML mangling. Recommended.

 - Option B: Recreate as proper HTML via the right GAS param. Looks slightly nicer in Gmail web, but the Outreach extension is still going to fight us for this contact.

 You'll pick A or B, then I do it. Sound good — should I read the GAS bridge code and pull the original draft body now?

 ❯ ill do whatever option you think is best

 ⏺ Going with Option A (plain text). Bypasses both the HTML rendering bug AND the Outreach extension's HTML mangling. Let me verify the GAS bridge signatures.

 ⎿  Loaded tools/CLAUDE.md

 ⏺ I have what I need. The fix is straightforward — gas.create_draft defaults to plain text when content_type isn't passed. The Session 52 bug was almost certainly that I passed HTML body but forgot

 content_type="text/html" , so Gmail stored the markup literally.

 Let me read the approved tone memory and the original draft body so I can rewrite faithfully.

 Step 1 — You delete the broken draft (10-second manual)

 Open Gmail Drafts, find the "Congrats on the Prop 3 win" draft to robert.burton@tulsaschools.org, click trash. One click, zero risk. I'd write a programmatic delete but gas_bridge.py doesn't expose

 delete_draft or list_drafts yet, and adding both methods + finding the draft_id by subject is more code than this is worth right now.

 Step 2 — I create a fresh plain-text draft via GAS

 Same recipient, same subject, body below. Plain text means real \n\n paragraph breaks, no <br> , no HTML entities. The Outreach extension can't mangle plain text, and Gmail web will render it cleanly. Here's

 the proposed body — verbatim, this is what I'll send to GAS:

 Robert,

 Steven at CodeCombat. We power CS/coding & safe AI curriculum for ~1,300 K-12 districts — Python, JS, AI, CTE pathways, all standards-aligned, teachers don't need a CS background to run it. Since a chunk of

 Prop 3 is earmarked for software subscriptions, figured I'd throw our hat in early while you're mapping out the RFP and vendor list.

 - Share some data, a few case studies, or efficacy reports.

 Best,

 Steven

 ⏺ Draft scheduled — nice. Back on the Session 53 rails.

 Next up: Stage 4 — rebuild signals. Three scanners, sequential (so we can spot-check each before moving on), total ~30 min and ~$1.30.

 Here's what I want to do first — /signal_funding :

 What it does

 F4 funding scanner. Serper web search + Claude Haiku extraction to find districts that recently received CS/STEM grants (state CS funding, NSF, private foundations, etc.). HIGH-confidence hits auto-queue to
 the Prospecting Queue as cs_funding_recipient strategy (priority tier 800-899). MEDIUM/LOW write to Signals tab only.

 Why it's first

 It's the cleanest test of the Session 52 Commit 1 fix . If the fix works, HIGH-confidence auto-queued rows will show non-zero Est. Enrollment in queue columns 15-17 (pulled via
 territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment ). If the fix is broken, those columns will be 0 and priority will collapse to tier base. Before Session 52 this was broken for every Session 49 + 51 strategy.

 What I'll run

 /signal_funding via Telegram — you trigger it from your phone or desktop Telegram. I can't call execute_tool directly from Claude Code (Scout's tools only run in the Railway process), so you send the command
 and we wait 3-5 min for results to post back to the Telegram chat. Cost ~$0.30-$0.50.

 Spot-check gates (when results post)

 Paste the Telegram output back here and I'll verify:

 1. Source URL column populated on every signal row (not blank, not placeholder) — proves the Session 51 URL-preservation fix is still holding

 2. Auto-queued HIGH rows show non-zero Est. Enrollment — proves Commit 1's add_district(**kwargs) fix is live on Railway

 3. Top 3 results sanity-check — I'll read the headlines and flag anything that looks hallucinated or weak (e.g., vendor marketing pages, student-level awards miscategorized as district grants, expired funding
 from years ago)

 4. Spot-check one URL against the sheet — you open a BoardDocs/news link, I verify the extracted district+dollar amount matches what's actually on the page

 1. Source URL column populated on every signal row (not blank, not placeholder) — proves the Session 51 URL-preservation fix is still holding
 2 Auto-queued HIGH rows show non-zero Est. Enrollment — proves Commit 1's add_district(**kwargs) fix is live on Railway
 3 Top 3 r sults sanity-check — I'll read the headlines and flag anything that looks hallucinated or weak (e.g., vendor marketing pages, student-level awards miscategorized as district grants, expired funding
 from years ago)
 4. Spot-check one URL against the sheet — you open a BoardDocs/news link, I verify the extracted district+dollar amount matches what's actually on the page

 If gates fail

 I stop, diagnose, and we fix before moving to /signal_competitors . No autopilot.

 If gates pass

 We move to /signal_competitors (F2 competitor displacement) next, same drill.

 ---

 Ready to fire /signal_funding in Telegram? If yes, send it and paste the output back here when it lands. If you want to do something else first (review the draft one more time, change the sequence order, skip
 F4 and go straight to F2, etc.), tell me.

 274 - ## REPO STRUCTURE

 274 + ## REFERENCE MATERIAL → docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md

 276 - ```

 277 - firstcocoagent/

 278 - ├── CLAUDE.md ← This file (project-wide rules)

 279 - ├── SCOUT_HISTORY.md ← Bug log + changelog (not loaded each session)

 280 - ├── Procfile ← "web: python -m agent.main"

 281 - ├── requirements.txt

 282 - ├── agent/

 283 - │ ├── CLAUDE.md ← Module APIs for agent/ files

 284 - │ ├── main.py ← Entry point. Scheduler poll loop. All tool dispatch.

 285 - │ ├── config.py ← Env vars

 286 - │ ├── claude_brain.py ← Claude API + tool definitions + memory injection

 287 - │ ├── memory_manager.py ← Persistent memory: read/write/GitHub commit

 288 - │ ├── scheduler.py ← CST-aware Scheduler class, check() only

 289 - │ ├── keywords.py ← Lead research title/keyword list

 290 - │ ├── voice_trainer.py ← Paginated email fetch + paired context analysis

 291 - │ ├── call_processor.py ← Transcript → summary → Google Doc

 292 - │ └── webhook_server.py ← aiohttp server for Fireflies webhook

 293 - ├── tools/

 294 - │ ├── CLAUDE.md ← Module APIs for tools/ files

 295 - │ ├── telegram_bot.py

 296 - │ ├── research_engine.py ← Research Job, Research Queue (singleton: research_queue)

 297 - │ ├── contact_extractor.py

 298 - │ ├── sheets_writer.py ← MODULE not class. write_contacts(), count_leads(), etc.

 299 - │ ├── gas_bridge.py ← GASBridge class

 300 - │ ├── github_pusher.py ← push_file(), list_repo_files(), get_file_content()

 301 - │ ├── sequence_builder.py ← build_sequence(), write_sequence_to_doc()

 302 - │ ├── activity_tracker.py ← MODULE not class. log_activity(), sync_gmail_activities()

 303 - │ ├── csv_importer.py ← MODULE not class. import_accounts(), classify_account()

 304 - │ ├── daily_call_list.py ← MODULE not class. build_daily_call_list()

 305 - │ ├── district_prospector.py ← MODULE not class. discover_districts(), suggest_upward_targets()

 306 - │ ├── lead_importer.py ← MODULE not class. import_leads(), import_contacts(), enrich

 307 - │ ├── proximity_engine.py ← MODULE not class. C5: proximity search, ESA mapping

 308 - │ ├── signal_processor.py ← MODULE not class. Signal intelligence: Gmail parsing, classification, scoring

 309 - │ ├── email_drafter.py ← MODULE not class. Auto-draft Gmail replies in Steven's voice

 310 - │ ├── charter_prospector.py ← F6 MODULE. Charter CMO seed list loader + queue functions

 311 - │ ├── cte_prospector.py ← F7 MODULE. CTE center seed list loader + queue functions

 312 - │ ├── private_schools.py ← F8 MODULE. Private school Serper discovery + diocesan/chain seed

 313 - │ ├── compliance_gap_scanner.py ← F9 MODULE. Serper PDF + Claude Sonnet document input (CA/IL/MA pilot)

 314 - │ └── fireflies.py ← Fireflies Client, Fireflies Error

 315 - ├── gas/

 316 - │ ├── CLAUDE.md ← GAS deployment checklist and gotchas

 317 - │ └── Code.gs ← Deployed at script.google.com as "Scout Bridge"

 318 - ├── prompts/

 319 - │ ├── system.md

 320 - │ ├── morning_brief.md

 321 - │ ├── eod_report.md

 322 - │ ├── sequence_templates.md ← 18 archetypes

 323 - │ └── reply_draft.md ← Email drafting workflow instructions

 324 - ├── memory/

 325 - │ ├── preferences.md

 326 - │ ├── context_summary.md

 327 - │ ├── voice_profile.md

 328 - │ ├── response_playbook.md ← 14 reply categories from 150+ emails

 329 - │ ├── draft_log.md ← Draft tracking for learning loop

 330 - │ └── sequence_building_rules.md

 331 - └── docs/

 332 - ├── CHANGELOG.md

 333 - ├── DECISIONS.md

 334 - ├── SETUP.md

 335 - └── SETUP_PHASE5.md

 336 - ```

 276 + The repo tree, full Railway env var table, the Claude tool registry (25 tools), and the complete Telegram shorthand command list (~80 commands) all live in **`docs/SCOUT_REFERENCE.md`**. Read i

 + t on demand — it's gitted, greppable, and not loaded into every session. CLAUDE.md was hitting the 40k char performance ceiling so this static reference material was extracted in Session 53.

 340 - ## RAILWAY ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES

 341 -

 342 - | Variable | Notes |

 343 - |----------|-------|

 344 - | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | Claude API |

 345 - | TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN | Bot token |

 346 - | TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID | 8677984089 |

 347 - | MORNING_BRIEF_TIME | 09:15 |

 348 - | EOD_REPORT_TIME | 16:30 |

 349 - | TIMEZONE | America/Chicago |

 350 - | AGENT_NAME | Scout |

 351 - | GITHUB_TOKEN | Fine-grained PAT, contents:write |

 352 - | GITHUB_REPO | scadkin/firstcocoagent |

 353 - | CHECKIN_START_HOUR | 10 |

 354 - | CHECKIN_END_HOUR | 16 |

 355 - | SERPER_API_KEY | serper.dev |

 356 - | GOOGLE_SHEETS_ID | From Sheet URL |

 357 - | GOOGLE_SHEETS_TERRITORY_ID | Separate sheet for territory data (falls back to main sheet) |

 358 - | GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON | Full JSON string, personal account |

 359 - | GAS_WEBHOOK_URL | **Update every time Code.gs gets new deployment** |

 360 - | GAS_SECRET_TOKEN | Must match Code.gs |

 361 - | FIREFLIES_API_KEY | app.fireflies.ai → Account → API |

 362 - | FIREFLIES_WEBHOOK_SECRET | Must match Fireflies webhook config |

 363 - | PRECALL_BRIEF_FOLDER_ID | Google Drive folder ID |

 364 - | SEQUENCES_FOLDER_ID | Google Drive folder ID. Paste full browser URL — query params stripped automatically. |

 365 -

 366 - **Note:** Phase 5+ env vars (`FIREFLIES_API_KEY`, `FIREFLIES_WEBHOOK_SECRET`, `PRECALL_BRIEF_FOLDER_ID`, `SEQUENCES_FOLDER_ID`) are read via `os.environ.get()` directly — NOT in `config.py`.

 370 - ## CLAUDE TOOLS (25 total, defined in claude_brain.py, handled in main.py)

 371 -

 372 - `research_district`, `get_sheet_status`, `get_research_queue_status`, `train_voice`, `draft_email`, `save_draft_to_gmail`, `get_calendar`, `log_call`, `create_district_deck`, `push_code`, `list

 - _repo_files`, `get_file_content`, `build_sequence`, `ping_gas_bridge`, `grade_draft`, `add_template`, `process_call_transcript`, `get_pre_call_brief`, `get_activity_summary`, `get_accounts_stat

 - us`, `set_goal`, `sync_gmail_activities`, `generate_call_list`, `discover_prospects`, `find_nearby_prospects`

 374 - ---

 375 -

 376 - ## SHORTHAND COMMANDS (handle_message in main.py)

 377 -

 378 - | Command | Action |

 379 - |---------|--------|

 380 - | `research [district], [state]`, `look up [district] in [state]` | direct dispatch to research_district — bypasses Claude |

 381 - | `proximity [state] [miles]`, `nearby districts in [state]` | find districts near active accounts (C5) |

 382 - | `esa [state]`, `service centers in [state]` | ESA/ESC region opportunities (C5) |

 383 - | `/signals` | top 5 district signals by heat score |

 384 - | `/signals all`, `/signals [state]`, `/signals new` | all signals, state filter, new only |

 385 - | `/signal_info N` | full detail on signal #N from last `/signals` list |

 386 - | `/signal_act N` | fast-path: mark acted → add to Prospecting Queue → auto-research |

 387 - | `/signal_dismiss N` | archive signal (hidden from default view) |

 388 - | `/signal_scan` | trigger manual signal scan of Gmail |

 389 - | `/signal_stats` | signal counts by type and state |

 390 - | `/signal_rss` | manual RSS-only scan (K-12 Dive, eSchool News, CSTA) |

 391 - | `/signal_board` | manual BoardDocs agenda scan (25 districts) |

 392 - | `/signal_bonds` | manual Ballotpedia bond measure scan |

 393 - | `/signal_leadership` | manual superintendent change scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 394 - | `/signal_rfp` | manual CS/STEM RFP opportunity scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 395 - | `/signal_legislation` | manual CS/STEM education legislation scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 396 - | `/signal_grants` | manual CS/STEM grant-funded district scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 397 - | `/signal_budget` | manual budget cycle/procurement signal scan (Serper + Claude, ~$0.03) |

 398 - | `/signal_algebra` | AI Algebra campaign: find districts adopting math/algebra curriculum (~$0.03) |

 399 - | `/signal_cyber` | Cybersecurity pre-launch: find districts with CTE cyber programs (~$0.03) |

 400 - | `/prospect_lookalike [state]` | find districts demographically similar to best customers ($0) |

 401 - | `/prospect_reengagement` | scan ALL Outreach sequences for finished/no-reply prospects |

 402 - | `/signal_roles [state]` | find CS/CTE/STEM leaders via Serper (~$2.50/scan, on-demand) |

 403 - | `/signal_csta` | find CSTA chapter leaders/members (~$1.20/scan, on-demand) |

 404 - | `/dormant [days]` | show accounts with past activity that went silent (default 90 days) |

 405 - | `/export_sequence [name]` | export Outreach sequence to Google Doc |

 406 - | `/ping_gas`, `ping gas`, `test gas` | ping GAS bridge |

 407 - | `/train_voice`, `train voice` | train voice from Gmail (24 months) |

 408 - | `/grade_draft`, `grade draft` | feedback on last draft → updates voice_profile.md |

 409 - | `/add_template [content]` | add template to voice profile |

 410 - | `/list_files`, `/ls` | list repo files |

 411 - | `/push_code [filepath]` | fetch-first: read file, ask for changes, edit + push |

 412 - | `/build_sequence [name]` | hybrid: Claude asks questions, then builds + sends directly |

 413 - | `looks good`, `save it`, `approved` | save pending draft to Gmail |

 414 - | `add email: addr@domain.org` | set recipient on pending draft |

 415 - | `/brief [meeting name]` | manual pre-call brief |

 416 - | `/recent_calls [num]` | recent external calls (1–20) |

 417 - | `/call [id] [email]` | post-call processing. Optional email override. |

 418 - | `/progress` or `/kpi` | today's activity vs KPI goals |

 419 - | `/sync_activities` | scan Gmail for PandaDoc + Dialpad events |

 420 - | `/set_goal [type] [target]` | update KPI target |

 421 - | `/call_list [N]` | generate daily call list (default 10, max 50) |

 422 - | `/color_leads` | recolor Leads tab rows by email confidence |

 423 - | `/eod` | manually trigger end-of-day report (useful on weekends) |

 424 - | `/draft_emails`, `draft my emails` | manually trigger email auto-drafting (also runs every 5 min during business hours) |

 425 - | `/draft [name]` | force-draft a specific sender's unread email, bypassing Haiku classification (e.g. `/draft Allison`) |

 426 - | `/list_charter_cmos [state]` | read-only view of the 43-CMO seed list (F6) |

 427 - | `/prospect_charter_cmos [state]` | queue charter CMOs from the seed list as `charter_cmo` strategy prospects (F6) |

 428 - | `/list_cte_centers [state]` | read-only view of the 79-CTE-center seed list (F7) |

 429 - | `/prospect_cte_centers [state]` | queue CTE centers from the seed list as `cte_center` strategy prospects (F7) |

 430 - | `/discover_coops [state]` | F10 Serper-based homeschool co-op discovery for a state |

 431 - | `/discover_private_schools [state]` | F8 Serper-based private school discovery for a state |

 432 - | `/prospect_private_networks [state]` | F8 queue the 24 diocesan/chain networks as `private_school_network` strategy |

 433 - | `/scan_compliance [state]` | F9 PDF pilot — CS graduation compliance gap scan. Pilot states: CA, IL, MA. Cost ~$0.50-$2/scan. |

 434 - | `/prospect_discover [state]` | cold district search via Serper |

 435 - | `/prospect_upward` | upward targets from active accounts |

 436 - | `/prospect` | show next 5 pending districts |

 437 - | `/prospect_all` | full queue grouped by status |

 438 - | `/prospect_winback` | scan Closed Lost tab for winback targets (last 12 months) |

 439 - | `/prospect_add [name], [state]` | manually add district to queue |

 440 - | `/prospect_approve 1,3,5` | approve from last batch, auto-queue research |

 441 - | `/prospect_skip 2,4` | skip from last batch |

 442 - | `/prospect_clear` | wipe all entries from Prospecting Queue tab |

 443 - | `/import_clear` | next CSV upload will clear & rewrite (then resets to merge) |

 444 - | `/import_merge` | switch CSV upload back to merge mode (default) |

 445 - | `/import_replace_state CA` | next CSV upload replaces only that state's rows; all other states untouched (then resets) |

 446 - | `/dedup_accounts` | Remove duplicate rows from Active Accounts tab (uses Name Key + State composite key — fixed Session 18) |

 447 - | `/import_leads` | next CSV upload imports as Salesforce leads (SF Leads tab) |

 448 - | `/import_contacts` | next CSV upload imports as Salesforce contacts (SF Contacts tab) |

 449 - | `/enrich_leads` | run Serper enrichment on unenriched SF Leads (add `contacts` arg for SF Contacts) |

 450 - | `/clear_leads` | clear SF Leads + Leads Assoc Active Accounts data rows + shrink grid |

 451 - | `/clear_contacts` | clear SF Contacts + Contacts Assoc Active Accounts data rows + shrink grid |

 452 - | `/territory_sync [state]` | download NCES territory data for one state or all |

 453 - | `/territory_stats [state]` | territory coverage summary (districts, schools, enrollment) |

 454 - | `/territory_gaps <state>` | gap analysis: cross-ref territory vs Active Accounts + Prospecting Queue |

 455 - | `/territory_map [state]` | generate interactive Folium territory map → Telegram file attachment |

 456 - | `/pipeline` | show open pipeline summary with stale alerts |

 457 - | `/pipeline_import` | next CSV upload imports as opportunities (Pipeline tab) |

 458 - | `/import_closed_lost` | next CSV upload imports as closed-lost opps (Closed Lost tab) |

 459 - | send a `.csv` file | Auto-detects opp vs lead vs contact vs account CSV; or Salesforce active accounts import (merge by default) |

 460 - | describe CSV before upload | "these are my salesforce leads" / "contacts from salesforce" / "pipeline opps" — sets routing for next CSV upload |

 461 - | caption on CSV upload | Same as above — type description as caption when sending the file |

 Now — the Robert Burton draft

 I see what's wrong, and it's worse than just visual: the draft body was saved as plain text containing literal HTML ( <br> tags + &#39; entities showing through). On top of that, the Outreach Chrome extension

 is hijacking the Gmail compose window for robert.burton@tulsaschools.org because he's a contact in Outreach — that orange "Prospect will be created when sending email" banner confirms it. This is the

 documented "Outreach extension strips draft body for contacts in Outreach" issue from CLAUDE.md, but with an extra layer: the underlying Gmail draft itself is malformed.

 Root cause hypothesis: When I created the draft via GAS bridge in Session 52, I almost certainly called gas.create_draft(to, subject, body=...) and passed an HTML-formatted body to the plain-text body

 parameter, when GAS's create Draft needs html Body=... for HTML content. Gmail then stored the literal <br> and &#39; strings as plain text, and any client (Gmail web, Outreach extension) renders them literally.

 Before I fix anything, I want to verify two things and propose options — not act:

 1. Verify the GAS create Draft signature so I know whether the right fix is html Body= , a different param name, or "rewrite as plain text with real line breaks." I'll read tools/gas_bridge.py and gas/Code.gs .

 2. Confirm the original approved copy from Session 52 so the rewrite uses the exact tone Steven approved (not a paraphrase).

 Then I'll come back with two options:

 - Option A: Delete the broken draft via GAS delete_draft and recreate it as a clean plain-text draft (real \n\n paragraph breaks, no HTML at all). Safest — bypasses both the HTML issue AND the Outreach

 extension's HTML mangling. Recommended.

 - Option B: Recreate as proper HTML via the right GAS param. Looks slightly nicer in Gmail web, but the Outreach extension is still going to fight us for this contact.

 You'll pick A or B, then I do it. Sound good — should I read the GAS bridge code and pull the original draft body now?

 ❯ ill do whatever option you think is best

 ⏺ Going with Option A (plain text). Bypasses both the HTML rendering bug AND the Outreach extension's HTML mangling. Let me verify the GAS bridge signatures.

 ⎿  Loaded tools/CLAUDE.md

 ⏺ I have what I need. The fix is straightforward — gas.create_draft defaults to plain text when content_type isn't passed. The Session 52 bug was almost certainly that I passed HTML body but forgot

 content_type="text/html" , so Gmail stored the markup literally.

 Let me read the approved tone memory and the original draft body so I can rewrite faithfully.

 ⏺ Draft scheduled — nice. Back on the Session 53 rails.

 Next up: Stage 4 — rebuild signals. Three scanners, sequential (so we can spot-check each before moving on), total ~30 min and ~$1.30.

 Here's what I want to do first — /signal_funding :

 What it does

 F4 funding scanner. Serper web search + Claude Haiku extraction to find districts that recently received CS/STEM grants (state CS funding, NSF, private foundations, etc.). HIGH-confidence hits auto-queue to

 the Prospecting Queue as cs_funding_recipient strategy (priority tier 800-899). MEDIUM/LOW write to Signals tab only.

 Why it's first

 It's the cleanest test of the Session 52 Commit 1 fix . If the fix works, HIGH-confidence auto-queued rows will show non-zero Est. Enrollment in queue columns 15-17 (pulled via

 territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment ). If the fix is broken, those columns will be 0 and priority will collapse to tier base. Before Session 52 this was broken for every Session 49 + 51 strategy.

 What I'll run

 /signal_funding via Telegram — you trigger it from your phone or desktop Telegram. I can't call execute_tool directly from Claude Code (Scout's tools only run in the Railway process), so you send the command

 and we wait 3-5 min for results to post back to the Telegram chat. Cost ~$0.30-$0.50.

 Spot-check gates (when results post)

 Paste the Telegram output back here and I'll verify:

 1. Source URL column populated on every signal row (not blank, not placeholder) — proves the Session 51 URL-preservation fix is still holding

 2. Auto-queued HIGH rows show non-zero Est. Enrollment — proves Commit 1's add_district(**kwargs) fix is live on Railway

 3. Top 3 results sanity-check — I'll read the headlines and flag anything that looks hallucinated or weak (e.g., vendor marketing pages, student-level awards miscategorized as district grants, expired funding

 from years ago)

 4. Spot-check one URL against the sheet — you open a BoardDocs/news link, I verify the extracted district+dollar amount matches what's actually on the page

 If gates fail

 I stop, diagnose, and we fix before moving to /signal_competitors . No autopilot.

 If gates pass

 We move to /signal_competitors (F2 competitor displacement) next, same drill.

 ---

 F4 and go straight to F2, etc.), tell me.

 I see what's wrong, and it's worse than just visual: the draft body was saved as plain text containing literal HTML ( <br> tags + &#39; entities showing through). On top of that, the Outreach Chrome extension

 is hijacking the Gmail compose window for robert.burton@tulsaschools.org because he's a contact in Outreach — that orange "Prospect will be created when sending email" banner confirms it. This is the

 documented "Outreach extension strips draft body for contacts in Outreach" issue from CLAUDE.md, but with an extra layer: the underlying Gmail draft itself is malformed.

 Root cause hypothesis: When I created the draft via GAS bridge in Session 52, I almost certainly called gas.create_draft(to, subject, body=...) and passed an HTML-formatted body to the plain-text body

 parameter, when GAS's create Draft needs html Body=... for HTML content. Gmail then stored the literal <br> and &#39; strings as plain text, and any client (Gmail web, Outreach extension) renders them literally.

 Before I fix anything, I want to verify two things and propose options — not act:

 1. Verify the GAS create Draft signature so I know whether the right fix is html Body= , a different param name, or "rewrite as plain text with real line breaks." I'll read tools/gas_bridge.py and gas/Code.gs .

 extension's HTML mangling. Recommended.

 ⏺ Draft scheduled — nice. Back on the Session 53 rails.

 Next up: Stage 4 — rebuild signals. Three scanners, sequential (so we can spot-check each before moving on), total ~30 min and ~$1.30.

 Here's what I want to do first — /signal_funding :

 What it does

 F4 funding scanner. Serper web search + Claude Haiku extraction to find districts that recently received CS/STEM grants (state CS funding, NSF, private foundations, etc.). HIGH-confidence hits auto-queue to

 the Prospecting Queue as cs_funding_recipient strategy (priority tier 800-899). MEDIUM/LOW write to Signals tab only.

 Why it's first

 It's the cleanest test of the Session 52 Commit 1 fix . If the fix works, HIGH-confidence auto-queued rows will show non-zero Est. Enrollment in queue columns 15-17 (pulled via

 territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment ). If the fix is broken, those columns will be 0 and priority will collapse to tier base. Before Session 52 this was broken for every Session 49 + 51 strategy.

 What I'll run

 /signal_funding via Telegram — you trigger it from your phone or desktop Telegram. I can't call execute_tool directly from Claude Code (Scout's tools only run in the Railway process), so you send the command

 and we wait 3-5 min for results to post back to the Telegram chat. Cost ~$0.30-$0.50.

 Spot-check gates (when results post)

 Paste the Telegram output back here and I'll verify:

 1. Source URL column populated on every signal row (not blank, not placeholder) — proves the Session 51 URL-preservation fix is still holding

 2. Auto-queued HIGH rows show non-zero Est. Enrollment — proves Commit 1's add_district(**kwargs) fix is live on Railway

 3. Top 3 results sanity-check — I'll read the headlines and flag anything that looks hallucinated or weak (e.g., vendor marketing pages, student-level awards miscategorized as district grants, expired funding

 from years ago)

 4. Spot-check one URL against the sheet — you open a BoardDocs/news link, I verify the extracted district+dollar amount matches what's actually on the page

 If gates fail

 I stop, diagnose, and we fix before moving to /signal_competitors . No autopilot.

 If gates pass

 We move to /signal_competitors (F2 competitor displacement) next, same drill.

 2 thought for 15s)

 ⏺ Found the failure mode. It's not a Session 52 regression — it's a pre-existing issue that was probably silently making this scanner look broken for a while.

 Railway logs (7:27-7:28 PM CST)

 00:27:02 Received: /signal_funding

 00:28:23 CS funding scan: 456 articles from 13 states ← Serper worked great
 00:28:23 CS funding scan: 0 raw, 0 signals, 0 queued ← Claude extracted nothing

 Gap between logs: 1.3 seconds. That's a complete Claude Haiku roundtrip. No error log in between, which rules out:

 - Claude API failure (would log "Claude CS funding extraction failed: ..." )
 - No JSON array in response (would log "no JSON array in response" )

 - JSON parse error (would log "JSON parse failed" )

 - Claude API failure (would log "Claude CS funding extraction failed: ..." )
 No JSON array in resp nse (would log "no JSON array in response" )
 JSON parse error (would log "JSON parse failed" )

 So Claude returned a valid but empty [] . The Serper leg is fine — it pulled 456 articles. The extraction prompt looked at them and found zero K-12 district CS funding awards.

 Most likely root cause (two candidates, probably both)

 1. The truncation window is way too small. Look at tools/signal_processor.py:3242 :

 combined_text = combined_text[: 14000 ]

 combined_text = combined_text[: 14000 ]

 456 articles × ~300 chars each (title + snippet + URL) = ~137K chars of raw input. The [:14000] cap keeps only the first ~10% — whatever happened to land at the top of the list after 13 states × 4-5 queries
 each were concatenated. If the first 40-50 articles are all state-program announcements (no specific recipient) rather than "District X awarded $Y" stories, Claude legitimately extracts zero. The rest of the
 456 articles never reach Claude at all.

 456 articles × ~300 chars each (title + snippet + URL) = ~137K chars of raw input. The [:14000] cap keeps only the first ~10% — whatever happened to land at the top of the list after 13 states × 4-5 queries
 each were concatenated. If the first 40-50 articles are all state-program announcements (no specific r cipient) rather than "District X aw rd d $Y" stories, Claude legi imately ex racts zero. The rest of the
 456 articles nev r r ach Claude at all.

 2. The EXCLUDE rules in the prompt are aggressive. Specifically "Awards >12 months old" combined with the fact that news articles often don't restate the award year in the snippet — Haiku plays it safe and
 drops them. Plus "Device/hardware-only grants" excludes anything CS-adjacent that mentions devices.

 Not a Session 52 regression

 Session 52 touched add_district(**kwargs) + lookup_district_enrollment + F9 Signals-only rewrite. Zero changes to scan_cs_funding_awards . This truncation + prompt combo has been in the code since F4 shipped in
 Session 49 and has likely been returning 0 or near-0 every run. We just never actually spot-checked until tonight.

 What I want to do next (need your call — not acting yet)

 Option A — Fast diagnostic, no code changes (~$0.01, 2 min):

 I write a one-shot local Python script that calls Serper with the same queries for IL only (not all 13 states), gets ~30-40 articles, then runs them through Claude Haiku with the current prompt. That tells us
 definitively: is it the truncation, the prompt, or the data itself. No Railway redeploy. No production impact. Tells us what Haiku actually returns.

 Option B — Instrument + redeploy (~5 min, ~$0.30 for a rescan):

 Add two log lines to the scanner: (1) first 500 chars of the Claude response before JSON parse, (2) first 10 article titles going into the prompt. Redeploy. You rerun /signal_funding . We see exactly what
 Claude saw and what it said back.

 Option C — Skip F4 for now, move to /signal_competitors :

 F4 is broken but not regressed — it's the same broken it's been. Don't let it block Stage 4. Come back to it with a proper fix session later (probably a real plan in plan mode since it's going to need prompt
 rewrite + truncation strategy changes + maybe a chunked multi-call approach).

 My recommendation: A, then C. A takes 2 minutes and gives us certainty. Then we park the fix (no rush — F4 has been returning 0 for a while and you haven't been missing anything the bot was supposed to catch),
 move to /signal_competitors , and come back to F4 in its own planning session because a real fix here is going to be 30-60 min of work and probably warrants a test harness.

 Which way? A+C, B, C alone, or something else?

 Now — the Robert Burton draft

 I see what's wrong, and it's worse than just visual: the draft body was saved as plain text containing literal HTML ( <br> tags + &#39; entities showing through). On top of that, the Outreach Chrome extension

 is hijacking the Gmail compose window for robert.burton@tulsaschools.org because he's a contact in Outreach — that orange "Prospect will be created when sending email" banner confirms it. This is the

 documented "Outreach extension strips draft body for contacts in Outreach" issue from CLAUDE.md, but with an extra layer: the underlying Gmail draft itself is malformed.

 Root cause hypothesis: When I created the draft via GAS bridge in Session 52, I almost certainly called gas.create_draft(to, subject, body=...) and passed an HTML-formatted body to the plain-text body

 parameter, when GAS's create Draft needs html Body=... for HTML content. Gmail then stored the literal <br> and &#39; strings as plain text, and any client (Gmail web, Outreach extension) renders them literally.

 Before I fix anything, I want to verify two things and propose options — not act:

 1. Verify the GAS create Draft signature so I know whether the right fix is html Body= , a different param name, or "rewrite as plain text with real line breaks." I'll read tools/gas_bridge.py and gas/Code.gs .

 2. Confirm the original approved copy from Session 52 so the rewrite uses the exact tone Steven approved (not a paraphrase).

 Then I'll come back with two options:

 - Option A: Delete the broken draft via GAS delete_draft and recreate it as a clean plain-text draft (real \n\n paragraph breaks, no HTML at all). Safest — bypasses both the HTML issue AND the Outreach

 extension's HTML mangling. Recommended.

 - Option B: Recreate as proper HTML via the right GAS param. Looks slightly nicer in Gmail web, but the Outreach extension is still going to fight us for this contact.

 Next up: Stage 4 — rebuild signals. Three scanners, sequential (so we can spot-check each before moving on), total ~30 min and ~$1.30.

 Here's what I want to do first — /signal_funding :

 What it does

 F4 funding scanner. Serper web search + Claude Haiku extraction to find districts that recently received CS/STEM grants (state CS funding, NSF, private foundations, etc.). HIGH-confidence hits auto-queue to

 the Prospecting Queue as cs_funding_recipient strategy (priority tier 800-899). MEDIUM/LOW write to Signals tab only.

 Why it's first

 It's the cleanest test of the Session 52 Commit 1 fix . If the fix works, HIGH-confidence auto-queued rows will show non-zero Est. Enrollment in queue columns 15-17 (pulled via

 territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment ). If the fix is broken, those columns will be 0 and priority will collapse to tier base. Before Session 52 this was broken for every Session 49 + 51 strategy.

 What I'll run

 /signal_funding via Telegram — you trigger it from your phone or desktop Telegram. I can't call execute_tool directly from Claude Code (Scout's tools only run in the Railway process), so you send the command

 and we wait 3-5 min for results to post back to the Telegram chat. Cost ~$0.30-$0.50.

 Spot-check gates (when results post)

 Paste the Telegram output back here and I'll verify:

 1. Source URL column populated on every signal row (not blank, not placeholder) — proves the Session 51 URL-preservation fix is still holding

 2. Auto-queued HIGH rows show non-zero Est. Enrollment — proves Commit 1's add_district(**kwargs) fix is live on Railway

 3. Top 3 results sanity-check — I'll read the headlines and flag anything that looks hallucinated or weak (e.g., vendor marketing pages, student-level awards miscategorized as district grants, expired funding

 from years ago)

 4. Spot-check one URL against the sheet — you open a BoardDocs/news link, I verify the extracted district+dollar amount matches what's actually on the page

 If gates fail

 I stop, diagnose, and we fix before moving to /signal_competitors . No autopilot.

 If gates pass

 We move to /signal_competitors (F2 competitor displacement) next, same drill.

 ⏺ Found the failure mode. It's not a Session 52 regression — it's a pre-existing issue that was probably silently making this scanner look broken for a while.

 Railway logs (7:27-7:28 PM CST)

 00:27:02 Received: /signal_funding

 00:28:23 CS funding scan: 456 articles from 13 states ← Serper worked great

 00:28:23 CS funding scan: 0 raw, 0 signals, 0 queued ← Claude extracted nothing

 Gap between logs: 1.3 seconds. That's a complete Claude Haiku roundtrip. No error log in between, which rules out:

 - Claude API failure (would log "Claude CS funding extraction failed: ..." )

 - No JSON array in response (would log "no JSON array in response" )

 - JSON parse error (would log "JSON parse failed" )

 So Claude returned a valid but empty [] . The Serper leg is fine — it pulled 456 articles. The extraction prompt looked at them and found zero K-12 district CS funding awards.

 Most likely root cause (two candidates, probably both)

 1. The truncation window is way too small. Look at tools/signal_processor.py:3242 :

 combined_text = combined_text[: 14000 ]

 456 articles × ~300 chars each (title + snippet + URL) = ~137K chars of raw input. The [:14000] cap keeps only the first ~10% — whatever happened to land at the top of the list after 13 states × 4-5 queries

 each were concatenated. If the first 40-50 articles are all state-program announcements (no specific recipient) rather than "District X awarded $Y" stories, Claude legitimately extracts zero. The rest of the

 456 articles never reach Claude at all.

 2. The EXCLUDE rules in the prompt are aggressive. Specifically "Awards >12 months old" combined with the fact that news articles often don't restate the award year in the snippet — Haiku plays it safe and

 drops them. Plus "Device/hardware-only grants" excludes anything CS-adjacent that mentions devices.

 Not a Session 52 regression

 Session 52 touched add_district(**kwargs) + lookup_district_enrollment + F9 Signals-only rewrite. Zero changes to scan_cs_funding_awards . This truncation + prompt combo has been in the code since F4 shipped in

 Session 49 and has likely been returning 0 or near-0 every run. We just never actually spot-checked until tonight.

 What I want to do next (need your call — not acting yet)

 Option A — Fast diagnostic, no code changes (~$0.01, 2 min):

 I write a one-shot local Python script that calls Serper with the same queries for IL only (not all 13 states), gets ~30-40 articles, then runs them through Claude Haiku with the current prompt. That tells us

 definitively: is it the truncation, the prompt, or the data itself. No Railway redeploy. No production impact. Tells us what Haiku actually returns.

 Option B — Instrument + redeploy (~5 min, ~$0.30 for a rescan):

 Add two log lines to the scanner: (1) first 500 chars of the Claude response before JSON parse, (2) first 10 article titles going into the prompt. Redeploy. You rerun /signal_funding . We see exactly what

 Claude saw and what it said back.

 Option C — Skip F4 for now, move to /signal_competitors :

 F4 is broken but not regressed — it's the same broken it's been. Don't let it block Stage 4. Come back to it with a proper fix session later (probably a real plan in plan mode since it's going to need prompt

 rewrite + truncation strategy changes + maybe a chunked multi-call approach).

 My recommendation: A, then C. A takes 2 minutes and gives us certainty. Then we park the fix (no rush — F4 has been returning 0 for a while and you haven't been missing anything the bot was supposed to catch),

 move to /signal_competitors , and come back to F4 in its own planning session because a real fix here is going to be 30-60 min of work and probably warrants a test harness.

 Which way? A+C, B, C alone, or something else?

 ⏺ Draft scheduled — nice. Back on the Session 53 rails.

 Next up: Stage 4 — rebuild signals. Three scanners, sequential (so we can spot-check each before moving on), total ~30 min and ~$1.30.

 Here's what I want to do first — /signal_funding :

 What it does

 F4 funding scanner. Serper web search + Claude Haiku extraction to find districts that recently received CS/STEM grants (state CS funding, NSF, private foundations, etc.). HIGH-confidence hits auto-queue to

 the Prospecting Queue as cs_funding_recipient strategy (priority tier 800-899). MEDIUM/LOW write to Signals tab only.

 Why it's first

 It's the cleanest test of the Session 52 Commit 1 fix . If the fix works, HIGH-confidence auto-queued rows will show non-zero Est. Enrollment in queue columns 15-17 (pulled via

 territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment ). If the fix is broken, those columns will be 0 and priority will collapse to tier base. Before Session 52 this was broken for every Session 49 + 51 strategy.

 What I'll run

 /signal_funding via Telegram — you trigger it from your phone or desktop Telegram. I can't call execute_tool directly from Claude Code (Scout's tools only run in the Railway process), so you send the command

 and we wait 3-5 min for results to post back to the Telegram chat. Cost ~$0.30-$0.50.

 Spot-check gates (when results post)

 Paste the Telegram output back here and I'll verify:

 1. Source URL column populated on every signal row (not blank, not placeholder) — proves the Session 51 URL-preservation fix is still holding

 2. Auto-queued HIGH rows show non-zero Est. Enrollment — proves Commit 1's add_district(**kwargs) fix is live on Railway

 3. Top 3 results sanity-check — I'll read the headlines and flag anything that looks hallucinated or weak (e.g., vendor marketing pages, student-level awards miscategorized as district grants, expired funding

 from years ago)

 4. Spot-check one URL against the sheet — you open a BoardDocs/news link, I verify the extracted district+dollar amount matches what's actually on the page

 If gates fail

 I stop, diagnose, and we fix before moving to /signal_competitors . No autopilot.

 If gates pass

 We move to /signal_competitors (F2 competitor displacement) next, same drill.

 ---

 Ready to fire /signal_funding in Telegram? If yes, send it and paste the output back here when it lands. If you want to do something else first (review the draft one more time, change the sequence order, skip

 F4 and go straight to F2, etc.), tell me.

 … +18 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

 ⏺ Found the failure mode. It's not a Session 52 regression — it's a pre-existing issue that was probably silently making this scanner look broken for a while.

 Railway logs (7:27-7:28 PM CST)

 00:27:02 Received: /signal_funding

 00:28:23 CS funding scan: 456 articles from 13 states ← Serper worked great

 00:28:23 CS funding scan: 0 raw, 0 signals, 0 queued ← Claude extracted nothing

 Gap between logs: 1.3 seconds. That's a complete Claude Haiku roundtrip. No error log in between, which rules out:

 - Claude API failure (would log "Claude CS funding extraction failed: ..." )

 - No JSON array in response (would log "no JSON array in response" )

 - JSON parse error (would log "JSON parse failed" )

 So Claude returned a valid but empty [] . The Serper leg is fine — it pulled 456 articles. The extraction prompt looked at them and found zero K-12 district CS funding awards.

 Most likely root cause (two candidates, probably both)

 1. The truncation window is way too small. Look at tools/signal_processor.py:3242 :

 combined_text = combined_text[: 14000 ]

 456 articles × ~300 chars each (title + snippet + URL) = ~137K chars of raw input. The [:14000] cap keeps only the first ~10% — whatever happened to land at the top of the list after 13 states × 4-5 queries

 each were concatenated. If the first 40-50 articles are all state-program announcements (no specific recipient) rather than "District X awarded $Y" stories, Claude legitimately extracts zero. The rest of the

 456 articles never reach Claude at all.

 2. The EXCLUDE rules in the prompt are aggressive. Specifically "Awards >12 months old" combined with the fact that news articles often don't restate the award year in the snippet — Haiku plays it safe and

 drops them. Plus "Device/hardware-only grants" excludes anything CS-adjacent that mentions devices.

 Not a Session 52 regression

 Session 52 touched add_district(**kwargs) + lookup_district_enrollment + F9 Signals-only rewrite. Zero changes to scan_cs_funding_awards . This truncation + prompt combo has been in the code since F4 shipped in

 Session 49 and has likely been returning 0 or near-0 every run. We just never actually spot-checked until tonight.

 What I want to do next (need your call — not acting yet)

 Option A — Fast diagnostic, no code changes (~$0.01, 2 min):

 I write a one-shot local Python script that calls Serper with the same queries for IL only (not all 13 states), gets ~30-40 articles, then runs them through Claude Haiku with the current prompt. That tells us

 definitively: is it the truncation, the prompt, or the data itself. No Railway redeploy. No production impact. Tells us what Haiku actually returns.

 Option B — Instrument + redeploy (~5 min, ~$0.30 for a rescan):

 Add two log lines to the scanner: (1) first 500 chars of the Claude response before JSON parse, (2) first 10 article titles going into the prompt. Redeploy. You rerun /signal_funding . We see exactly what

 Claude saw and what it said back.

 Option C — Skip F4 for now, move to /signal_competitors :

 F4 is broken but not regressed — it's the same broken it's been. Don't let it block Stage 4. Come back to it with a proper fix session later (probably a real plan in plan mode since it's going to need prompt

 rewrite + truncation strategy changes + maybe a chunked multi-call approach).

 My recommendation: A, then C. A takes 2 minutes and gives us certainty. Then we park the fix (no rush — F4 has been returning 0 for a while and you haven't been missing anything the bot was supposed to catch),

 move to /signal_competitors , and come back to F4 in its own planning session because a real fix here is going to be 30-60 min of work and probably warrants a test harness.

 Which way? A+C, B, C alone, or something else?

 the proposed body — verbatim, this is what I'll send to GAS:

 Robert,

 Steven at CodeCombat. We power CS/coding & safe AI curriculum for ~1,300 K-12 districts — Python, JS, AI, CTE pathways, all standards-aligned, teachers don't need a CS background to run it. Since a chunk of

 Prop 3 is earmarked for software subscriptions, figured I'd throw our hat in early while you're mapping out the RFP and vendor list.

 Steven

 CodeCombat / Ozaria

 content_type: none (= plain text)

 framing).

 ⏺ Draft scheduled — nice. Back on the Session 53 rails.

 Next up: Stage 4 — rebuild signals. Three scanners, sequential (so we can spot-check each before moving on), total ~30 min and ~$1.30.

 Here's what I want to do first — /signal_funding :

 What it does

 F4 funding scanner. Serper web search + Claude Haiku extraction to find districts that recently received CS/STEM grants (state CS funding, NSF, private foundations, etc.). HIGH-confidence hits auto-queue to

 the Prospecting Queue as cs_funding_recipient strategy (priority tier 800-899). MEDIUM/LOW write to Signals tab only.

 Why it's first

 It's the cleanest test of the Session 52 Commit 1 fix . If the fix works, HIGH-confidence auto-queued rows will show non-zero Est. Enrollment in queue columns 15-17 (pulled via

 territory_data.lookup_district_enrollment ). If the fix is broken, those columns will be 0 and priority will collapse to tier base. Before Session 52 this was broken for every Session 49 + 51 strategy.

 What I'll run

 /signal_funding via Telegram — you trigger it from your phone or desktop Telegram. I can't call execute_tool directly from Claude Code (Scout's tools only run in the Railway process), so you send the command

 and we wait 3-5 min for results to post back to the Telegram chat. Cost ~$0.30-$0.50.

 Spot-check gates (when results post)

 Paste the Telegram output back here and I'll verify:

 1. Source URL column populated on every signal row (not blank, not placeholder) — proves the Session 51 URL-preservation fix is still holding

 2. Auto-queued HIGH rows show non-zero Est. Enrollment — proves Commit 1's add_district(**kwargs) fix is live on Railway

 3. Top 3 results sanity-check — I'll read the headlines and flag anything that looks hallucinated or weak (e.g., vendor marketing pages, student-level awards miscategorized as district grants, expired funding

 from years ago)

 4. Spot-check one URL against the sheet — you open a BoardDocs/news link, I verify the extracted district+dollar amount matches what's actually on the page

 If gates fail

 I stop, diagnose, and we fix before moving to /signal_competitors . No autopilot.

 If gates pass

 We move to /signal_competitors (F2 competitor displacement) next, same drill.
